title: Conceptual Diagram of Interior Point and Interior of a Set
description: A visual representation of an interior point, the interior of a set, and its relationship to open balls and boundaries in a metric space.
elements: Open Ball B(p, r) around p
relationships: Point p is an interior point of A if there exists an open ball B(p, r) completely contained within A
The interior of A (A°) is the set of all interior points of A
A° is the largest open set contained in A
The boundary separates the interior from the exterior (complement) of A
